propositions: Experiment A re-judges N=50 findings from the G6/E4/T3 cohort with 3 votes each and measures self-consistency of the Pass-3 decision via Fleiss' kappa and raw agreement, passing iff kappa>=0.6 and raw>=0.8, else recorded as an R5 blocker.

Experiment B judges 14 plans (with >=3 top-level sections) over 3 distinct section-order permutations and measures verdict stability, passing iff kappa>=0.6 and raw>=0.8, else recorded as an R3 prerequisite failure.
E6 measures judge reliability (agreement across repeats) rather than accuracy against gold, avoiding the circular-adjudication error that invalidated E1's TP/FP labeling.

Infra-INDETERMINATE verdicts carrying coverage.llm_unavailable or coverage.verify_failed, and per-vote indeterminate decisions from missing Pass-2 verification, are dropped and re-run as execution failures, while genuine judge-INDETERMINATE is kept as its own agreement category.

Re-judge inputs are frozen to committed files and the agentic Pass-2 path requires explicit repo_root plus an active gate_read_root session; Exp B varies section order via a throwaway REBAR_TRACKER_DIR clone with sign=False/emit_sidecar=False/force=True.
Pure helpers fleiss_kappa, raw_agreement, and permute_sections are unit-tested with no LLM, verifying identity permutation, reproducible seeded distinct orderings, and content preservation.
